name: GANGLION CELL TUMOR
definition: rare benign tumor composed of mature ganglion cells LHERMITTE-DUCLOS DISEASE rare hamartomatous disorder uncommon slow-growing relatively benign tumor composed of glial + nerve cells DESMOPLASTIC INFANTILE ASTROCYTOMA = SUPERFICIAL CEREBRAL uncommon variety of ganglioglioma exclusively in infants

demographics.Histo: purely neuronal tumor composed of abnormal mature ganglion cells without
demographics.Location: floor of 3rd ventricle > temporal lobe > cerebellum > parietooccipital region >
demographics.Path: disruption of normal cerebellar laminar structure
demographics.Peak age: 10-20 years; in 80% < 30 years of age; M > F

dominant_finding: hydrocephalus ← compression of 4th ventricle + effacement of cerebellopontine angle
distribution: floor of 3rd ventricle > temporal lobe > cerebellum > parietooccipital region >
discriminating_features: variable (hypo- / isointense) nonspecific MR appearance on T1WI
CHARACTERISTIC extension of enhancement to leptomeningeal margin ← firm dural
hyperattenuating mass with little mass effect
hydrocephalus ← compression of 4th ventricle + effacement of cerebellopontine angle

CT: hyperattenuating mass with little mass effect
hydrocephalus ← compression of 4th ventricle + effacement of cerebellopontine angle
MR: iso- to hypointense on T1WI + T2WI
cerebellar mass with "striated cerebellum" sign = tiger-striped / corduroy laminated
hyper- and isointense relative to gray matter on T2WI
iso- and hypointense relative to gray matter on T1WI
little associated mass effect / vasogenic edema
variable (hypo- / isointense) nonspecific MR appearance on T1WI
commonly at least one hyperintense region on T2WI
cystic component may have higher signal intensity than CSF ← gelatinous material
slightly hyperattenuating solid portion typically located along cortical margin
CHARACTERISTIC extension of enhancement to leptomeningeal margin ← firm dural
XRAY: thinning of skull in occipital region

clinical.Rx: decompression of ventricles + resection of mass
